title: FLIP2 Protein Fitness Landscape Benchmark

data_urls.access_notes: Project site links paper, code, Zenodo, and downloads.
Paperclip extraction reported redistributed benchmark data under CC-BY 4.0.

instructions: Use the FLIP2 project site to select one dataset and split family before broader downloads.
Record dataset name, split category, split name, wild-type protein, mutation notation, sequence, and fitness column.
Use official number, position, mutation, fitness, and wild-type split families as operating points for SPECTRA curves.

scale.download_size: multiple protein fitness datasets; use one dataset/split family first

recommended_axes.name: mutation_number_generalization
recommended_axes.definition: Distance by number of mutations from wild type and from observed training variants.

recommended_axes.name: mutation_position_generalization
recommended_axes.definition: Support for positions or local sequence windows mutated in train versus test.

recommended_axes.name: mutation_identity_generalization
recommended_axes.definition: Support for specific amino-acid substitutions or substitution classes in training.

recommended_axes.name: wildtype_scaffold_generalization
recommended_axes.definition: Similarity across wild-type proteins, homologs, or scaffold families.

useful_for: Modern protein-engineering SPECTRA benchmark with explicit split categories.
Testing whether protein language models extrapolate to unseen mutations, positions, higher mutational burden, or wild types.
Dataset Scout examples where a benchmark already exposes multiple operating points.
leakage_risks: Within-landscape variants can share strong local mutational context.
Split category labels should be contextualized as operating points, not the complete curve.
Fitness split axes are post-hoc unless constructed with training-only targets.

paperclip_provenance.notes: Paperclip and official-source check identified FLIP2 as seven new protein fitness datasets with split categories for number, position, mutation, fitness, and wild-type generalization.
